note: Module adds HTML attributes to Layout Builder components via a contextual 'Manage attributes' form, a global settings config object, two permissions, and a preprocess/event-subscriber render path. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded); they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using Layout Builder, I want editors to add HTML attributes (id, class, style, data-*) to individual blocks placed in a layout. Which module does this, and what three parts of a block can attributes target?
evals.expected_output: The Layout Builder Component Attributes module. Attributes target three groups: the block wrapper (outer) element, the block title element, and the block content (inner) element.

evals.prompt: What module dependency does Layout Builder Component Attributes require to work on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: It depends only on core Layout Builder (drupal:layout_builder).

evals.prompt: How do I control which attribute types (id/class/style/data) editors are allowed to set on Layout Builder components? Give the config object and the three keys.
evals.expected_output: The layout_builder_component_attributes.settings config object, via the three group keys allowed_block_attributes, allowed_block_title_attributes, and allowed_block_content_attributes (each with id/class/style/data booleans, default true). Edit at /admin/config/content/layout-builder-component-attributes.

evals.prompt: What are the two permissions Layout Builder Component Attributes defines, and which one lets editors add attributes to components versus manage the global settings?
evals.expected_output: 'manage layout builder component attributes' lets editors add attributes to components (the per-component Manage attributes form); 'administer layout builder component attributes' gates the global settings form.

evals.prompt: As an editor, how do I actually add an id or class to a specific block on an entity's Layout Builder layout page?
evals.expected_output: On the layout page, use the component's 'Manage attributes' contextual link (an off-canvas form, shown after 'Configure'), enter id/class/style/data-* in the relevant group (Block / Block title / Block content), and click Update.

evals.prompt: How do the stored component attributes end up on the rendered block markup in Drupal? Which Twig attribute variables get the wrapper, title, and content attributes?
evals.expected_output: An event subscriber puts them on the build as #component_attributes; layout_builder_component_attributes_preprocess_block() merges each group into the block Twig variables: attributes (wrapper), title_attributes (title), and content_attributes (content).

evals.prompt: I added attributes to the block content (inner) element in Layout Builder Component Attributes but they aren't showing up. Why, and what does the theme need?
evals.expected_output: Content-group attributes only render if the active theme's block.html.twig outputs content_attributes on the inner element. Many core themes don't print it, so those attributes are dropped; wrapper and title attributes still render.

evals.prompt: In the Layout Builder Component Attributes 'Manage attributes' form, how do I enter data-* attributes, and what naming rule is enforced?
evals.expected_output: One data-* attribute per line in the textarea, using name|value with the value optional (e.g. data-test|example-value or data-flag). Each name must begin with 'data-'; a line without a value renders as a boolean attribute.
